comment.body:
I think the best way to describe the last few days (and this is how im reading it)  and what almas is saying is this. 

Lets say in the city of Miami everyone is expecting a hurricane to hit. They go out and buy hurricane insurance. The data points to a hurricane hitting so this is a no brainer right? Well while everyone is buying this insurance, the insurance provider is not raising rates. So everyone thinks they're getting a deal but not asking why the provider is not raising rates. Now the provider could be wrong in which case they will aggressively start bidding up the premiums.
comment.raw_body:

Now the provider could be wrong in which case they will aggressively start bidding up the premiums.

I’m trying to follow. 

What do you mean vix above vix futures?  

 VIX is the calculation off of the next 30 days’ options. 

Are you saying the first 30-day strip is above the rest of the forward curve?

Doesn’t that mean some large somebody was selling the forward curve driving down prices?
